feature: M3 §4 referee-as-lint — mechanize the constitution's 'No orphan rules, no jobless experts' invariant (constitution.md Rule-ownership section) as two NEW fail-level checks in scripts/lint-bundle.mjs: (1) no orphan rule — every numbered rule has exactly one _(owner: `key`)_ that is a roster key; (2) no jobless expert — every roster expert owns >=2 rules. BOTH gated on mined-ownership-present: skip when the STARTER banner ('> **veriloop STARTER**', render.mjs:108) or the TODO-owner placeholder strings (render.mjs:114,116) are present, so a pre-mining starter bundle never trips them. Add fixtures/mined-bundle (a complete, fully-owned bundle that lints clean) + selftests (owner-stripped -> fail; expert with 1 rule -> fail; starter bundle -> fails do NOT fire). Branch from main; patch version bump; must not regress lint-bundle exit 0 on veriloop's own bundle.

checks.name: test
checks.tail:
ok   - lint-bundle: a fresh STARTER bundle passes — the referee skips pre-mining (gate regression guard)
ok   - lint-bundle: the starter reports the referee was skipped (pre-mining gate fired)

220 ok, 0 failed

> veriloop@0.3.8 test
> node scripts/selftest.mjs

concerns: [baseline-reviewer (MODE: REVIEW) — senior-engineer correctness & craft, veriloop node bundle] The 0.3.8 changelog entry states "veriloop's own hand-owned constitution (code-review 1/2/3, security 4/5/6/7, drift 8/9/10) passes the new fails." That ownership layout is the NEW fixture's scheme, not veriloop's own. I verified the actual tags in .claude/veriloop/constitution.md are code-review = rules 1,2,10; security = 4,5,6,7; drift = 3,8,9 — which matches the OWNER'S SPEC ("code-review 1/2/10 ... drift 3/8/9"). The changelog therefore misdescribes the very artifact the MUST-NOT-REGRESS clause is about (it claims drift owns 8/9/10 when drift owns 3/8/9, and code-review owns 1/2/3 when it owns 1/2/10). This is a shipped, user-facing doc inaccuracy — CLAUDE.md §6 treats inaccurate behavior descriptions as block-merge. The code itself is correct (own bundle lints exit 0); only the prose is wrong. Note: the m3-plan.md implementation note correctly uses this same 1/2/3 // 8/9/10 layout to describe the FIXTURE, so the error is confined to the changelog's mislabeling of it as veriloop's own.
implSummary:
Implemented M3 §4 (referee-as-lint) on a worktree branched from main (c4d33c1, v0.3.7 → patch-bumped to 0.3.8). No commit/push.

WHAT SHIPPED:
- scripts/lint-bundle.mjs: new "section 8" (after the section-5 manifest block, before the report). Builds rosterKeys from the manifest, reads .claude/veriloop/constitution.md, and GATES on preMining = STARTER banner (render.mjs:108) OR either TODO-owner placeholder (render.mjs:114,116). Pre-mining → one ok('rule-ownership referee skipped — pre-mining STARTER bundle'), neither strict fail runs (the section-5 jobless-EVIDENCE warn is untouched). Once mined, two NEW fail-level checks: (a) NO ORPHAN RULE — the constitution is split into numbered-rule blocks (open on /^\d+\.\s/, close on the next such line or a heading/hr /^(#{1,6}\s|---\s*$)/, which correctly attributes owner tags on a rule's continuation line); each block must carry exactly one /_\(owner:\s*`([^`]+)`\)_/ tag whose key is a roster expert (zero / >1 / off-roster → fail). (b) NO JOBLESS EXPERT — every roster key owning <2 rules → fail. The existing gate-equality FAIL and jobless-evidence WARN are unchanged.
- fixtures/mined-bundle/ (NEW, 16 files): a complete, fully-owned post-mining bundle that lints clean. Built by generating a starter for a 3-expert roster {code-review, security, drift} (via interview.roster_add) then replacing only its constitution with a fully-owned 10-rule form (code-review 1/2/3, security 4/5/6/7, drift 8/9/10; no STARTER banner, no TODO placeholders). Its manifest veriloop_version stays 0.3.7 (truthful — the version that generated it).
- scripts/selftest.mjs: +cpSync import and 4 new tests (8 assertions) — positive fixture lints clean + reports referee ran; tmp copy with one owner tag stripped → exit 1 + /orphan rule/; tmp copy reassigning two drift tags → exit 1 + /jobless expert/; fresh STARTER bundle → exit 0 + /rule-ownership referee skipped/. Failure paths mutate tmp copies, never a committed broken fixture (constitution rule 3). Count 212 → 220.
- Version bump 0.3.7 → 0.3.8 across all five stamped locations (generate.mjs VERILOOP_VERSION, package.json, .claude-plugin/plugin.json, marketplace.json metadata+plugins[0]) + new CHANGELOG.md '## 0.3.8 — 2026-07-21' entry; also bumped repo-root .claude/veriloop/veriloop-manifest.json veriloop_version for truthfulness.
- docs/plans/m3-plan.md §4: appended an Implementation-notes paragraph reflecting what shipped.

NO-REGRESSION CONFIRMED: node scripts/lint-bundle.mjs on veriloop's own hand-owned bundle → exit 0 (referee RUNS and PASSES: roster {code-review,security,drift}, ownership 3/4/3, no banner). Fresh starter still skips. Did not touch mine.mjs/scan.mjs/generate.mjs roster logic.

lenses.lens: baseline-reviewer (MODE: REVIEW) — senior-engineer correctness & craft, veriloop node bundle
lenses.summary: The implementation is correct and spec-faithful; no blockers. I ran the real gate: `npm test` → 220 ok / 0 failed (baseline 207 → 215 assert() calls, count grew as required); `node scripts/lint-bundle.mjs` on veriloop's own bundle → exit 0 with the new \"rule-ownership referee — every rule owned...\" line firing (so the strict fails genuinely run and pass, not skip); `--bundle fixtures/mined-bundle` → exit 0. Verified correct: (1) both fail-level checks read the constitution's inline _(owner:`key`)_ tags against manifest roster keys; the orphan check splits the constitution into numbered-rule blocks on /^\\d+\\.\\s/ closing on the next rule or a heading/hr, which correctly attributes owner tags on continuation lines and never mis-reads the bottom \"### Rule ownership\" summary (those lines carry no _(owner:...)_ tags and sit past the closing ---). (2) The mined-ownership gate ORs the STARTER banner (render.mjs:108) and both TODO-owner placeholders (render.mjs:114/116) exactly as spec'd, so a pre-mining starter is exempt. (3) The hardcoded-literal coupling to render.mjs is genuinely guarded — the gate-skip selftest GENERATES a fresh starter and asserts the referee skips, so any drift in the banner/placeholder text would flip that starter to running-and-failing and break the test. (4) The two failure paths mutate tmp COPIES (orphan: strip rule 1's code-review tag → code-review still owns 2, only orphan fires; jobless: reassign 2 drift tags → drift drops to 1) rather than committing a second broken fixture, honoring constitution rule 3. (5) Existing jobless-evidence WARN and gate-equality FAIL are untouched; generate.mjs change is only the version constant, not roster logic (non-goal respected). (6) All veriloop version stamps agree at 0.3.8 (plugin.json, marketplace.json, manifest, package.json, generate.mjs). The only issue is a factual mislabel in the CHANGELOG describing veriloop's own constitution with the fixture's ownership scheme.

findings.issue: The 0.3.8 changelog entry states "veriloop's own hand-owned constitution (code-review 1/2/3, security 4/5/6/7, drift 8/9/10) passes the new fails." That ownership layout is the NEW fixture's scheme, not veriloop's own. I verified the actual tags in .claude/veriloop/constitution.md are code-review = rules 1,2,10; security = 4,5,6,7; drift = 3,8,9 — which matches the OWNER'S SPEC ("code-review 1/2/10 ... drift 3/8/9"). The changelog therefore misdescribes the very artifact the MUST-NOT-REGRESS clause is about (it claims drift owns 8/9/10 when drift owns 3/8/9, and code-review owns 1/2/3 when it owns 1/2/10). This is a shipped, user-facing doc inaccuracy — CLAUDE.md §6 treats inaccurate behavior descriptions as block-merge. The code itself is correct (own bundle lints exit 0); only the prose is wrong. Note: the m3-plan.md implementation note correctly uses this same 1/2/3 // 8/9/10 layout to describe the FIXTURE, so the error is confined to the changelog's mislabeling of it as veriloop's own.
findings.fix: In CHANGELOG.md:4 change the parenthetical to veriloop's real ownership: "(code-review 1/2/10, security 4/5/6/7, drift 3/8/9)".

findings.issue: The committed fixture's manifest is stamped "veriloop_version": "0.3.7" while this change bumps the repo to 0.3.8 (it was generated by the pre-bump generator). No check couples the fixture version to VERILOOP_VERSION, so lint/selftest stay green and there is no functional impact — but the spec's "all version stamps agree" intent reads slightly against a frozen fixture pinned to the prior version.
findings.fix: Optional: regenerate/edit the fixture manifest to 0.3.8 for tidiness, or leave as-is and treat the fixture as an intentionally frozen artifact. Harmless either way.

lenses.summary: PASS — no BLOCKER, no SHOULD-FIX from the security/supply-chain lens. The change is tightly scoped to scripts/lint-bundle.mjs (adds section 8, a pure read-only referee: readFileSync + regex, zero execution), a new selftest block, and version/doc stamps. Constitution rules 4–7 (the reviewer's beat) are untouched: detectors.mjs sanitization, verify.mjs safety tiers, and the hostile-ci scan-only covenant are not modified, and the new code executes nothing — the pre-existing workflow check uses `node --check` (static parse), so the new mined-bundle fixture's workflow is never run. Verified live in the worktree: (1) the three gate literals in the code match render.mjs:108/114/116 byte-for-byte; (2) `lint-bundle --bundle fixtures/mined-bundle` → exit 0 with the referee reported as RUN (not skipped); (3) `lint-bundle` on veriloop's own hand-owned bundle → exit 0 (code-review 1/2/10, security 4/5/6/7… all ≥2 owners, no self-regression); (4) `npm test` → exit 0, 220 ok / 0 failed, count grew from the 212 baseline (+8); (5) both negative selftests target the correct failure (orphan test strips rule 1's tag while code-review keeps 2 rules; jobless test reassigns 2 of drift's 3 tags leaving drift at 1) and mutate tmp copies, never a committed broken fixture (honors constitution rule 3); (6) the starter gate-skip is guarded by a live generate.mjs run, so a future render.mjs banner change would trip the selftest; (7) no secrets or .env files in the fixture. Two NITs, both in the fixture manifest metadata and non-load-bearing.

findings.issue: The fixture manifest's roster_notes says "security expert NOT added — no concrete auth/db/secret surface detected (avoids a jobless expert)", yet the roster (line ~14) DOES include the security expert with evidence, and the fixture constitution gives security rules 4/5/6/7. This is a stale artifact from the fixture's construction (generate a 1-expert starter, then hand-add security+drift and rewrite the constitution). No lint check cross-validates roster_notes against roster, so tests stay green — but the spec asks for a "complete, well-formed" bundle and this note self-contradicts the roster it ships beside. Not load-bearing for the referee.
findings.fix: Edit fixtures/mined-bundle/.claude/veriloop/veriloop-manifest.json to remove or correct the stale roster_notes entry so it matches the 3-expert roster the fixture actually ships (e.g. note that security was added for the auth/db/secret surface).

findings.issue: The committed fixture's internal veriloop_version is "0.3.7" while this change bumps the repo to 0.3.8. Harmless — the fixture is a frozen snapshot and lint-bundle does not check its version — but it means the fixture was captured one release behind and does not reflect a bundle a current veriloop would emit.
findings.fix: Optional: regenerate the fixture (or bump its manifest stamp) at 0.3.8 so the snapshot matches the emitting version. Leave as-is if fixtures are intentionally frozen artifacts.

lenses.lens: drift (MODE: REVIEW) — divergence from spec, plan, constitution, and prior work
lenses.summary: GO. The M3 §4 referee-as-lint change faithfully implements the owner's spec: two new fail-level checks (no orphan rule / no jobless expert) read the constitution's inline _(owner: `key`)_ tags against manifest roster keys, both gated on mined-ownership-present via the exact render.mjs:108/114/116 starter literals, with the existing jobless-evidence WARN and gate-equality FAIL untouched. Verified end-to-end: the mined fixture lints clean with the referee running (not skipped); stripping an owner tag → exit 1 (orphan); dropping an expert to 1 rule → exit 1 (jobless); a fresh STARTER bundle → exit 0 (gate skips); and veriloop's own hand-owned bundle → exit 0 with the referee running and passing (no self-regression). npm selftest is green (220 ok, 0 failed; assert count 207→215, grows from baseline). All version stamps agree at 0.3.8 (patch bump), and CHANGELOG + m3-plan.md are updated to match what shipped. No constitution invariant is violated; scope non-goals (mine/scan/generate roster logic, the WARN, the gate FAIL) are all respected. No BLOCKER or SHOULD-FIX; three NITs are latent-fragility / cosmetic only.

findings.issue: The block parser treats every line matching /^\d+\.\s/ as a new numbered rule. A constitution rule whose body contained an ordered sub-list (e.g. an indented "1. first sub-point") would be misparsed as a fresh top-level rule, corrupting ownership counts. Not triggered by any current bundle (the fixture and veriloop's own constitution have no ordered sub-lists), so it is a latent fragility, not an active bug.
findings.fix: Optional hardening: only open a new block on a line with no leading indentation (anchor with /^\d+\.\s/ against a de-indented line, or require column 0). Leave as-is if sub-lists are considered out of convention.

findings.issue: When a mined bundle has a non-empty constitution but an EMPTY roster (rosterKeys.size === 0), the referee block runs neither branch — it emits no ok and no fail, staying silent. Exit code is still 1 because section 5 already fails on an empty roster, so behavior is correct, but the referee produces no signal in that case.
findings.fix: Acceptable as-is (section 5 covers it). If desired, add an explicit branch that notes the referee could not run on an empty roster.

findings.issue: Fixture manifest is internally contradictory: roster_notes says 'security expert NOT added — no concrete auth/db/secret surface detected (avoids a jobless expert)', yet the roster DOES include a security expert, and emitted_files marks constitution.md ownership as 'starter' though its content is fully mined. Leftover copy-paste from the starter-then-rewrite construction. Not checked by any lint rule and does not affect the change, but it is a stale/misleading claim inside a committed artifact.
findings.fix: Trim the roster_notes line and set constitution ownership to 'hand' in the fixture manifest so the artifact reads truthfully.

implPreflight: npm test (node scripts/selftest.mjs) → 220 ok, 0 failed (exit 0; baseline was 212, so count grew +8). node scripts/lint-bundle.mjs --bundle <worktree> (veriloop's own bundle) → exit 0. node scripts/lint-bundle.mjs --bundle fixtures/mined-bundle → exit 0. version-stamp agreement assertion → all six locations report 0.3.8. node --check on lint-bundle.mjs, selftest.mjs, generate.mjs → all clean. No mutating commands run.
